instructions.presentation_valid: Does the case presentation match veterinary expectations?
instructions.urgency_valid: Is the assigned urgency level clinically appropriate?
instructions.must_not_miss: Are all critical red flags and must-not-miss conditions addressed?
instructions.questioning_valid: Is the questioning sequence clinically sound?
instructions.unknown_policy_valid: Are 'cannot assess' responses handled per policy?

cases.description: Blue gums with respiratory distress should immediately escalate.
cases.requestText: My dog is struggling to breathe and his gums look blue.

cases.description: Distended abdomen with unproductive retching should escalate.
cases.requestText: His belly is huge and tight and he keeps trying to vomit but nothing comes out.

cases.description: Male dog straining with no urine should escalate.
cases.requestText: My male dog keeps squatting and straining but almost no urine is coming out.

cases.description: Multiple seizures in one day should escalate.
cases.requestText: She has had two seizure episodes today and still seems out of it.

cases.description: Deep actively bleeding wound should escalate.
cases.requestText: He has a deep cut on his leg and it is bleeding a lot right now.

cases.description: Overheating with collapse signs should escalate.
cases.requestText: After a walk in the heat she is panting hard, weak, and her gums look bright red.

cases.description: Possible xylitol ingestion should escalate.
cases.requestText: My dog ate sugar-free gum and now she is vomiting and shaky.

cases.description: Trauma with inability to stand should escalate.
cases.requestText: He was hit by a car and now he cannot stand up.

cases.description: Stuck puppy during labor should escalate.
cases.requestText: My dog is in labor and a puppy seems stuck with hard straining.

cases.description: Nursing dam with tremors and weakness should escalate.
cases.requestText: She is nursing puppies and now she is trembling badly and seems weak and restless.

cases.description: Collapse with pale gums should escalate.
cases.requestText: He suddenly collapsed and his gums are very pale.

cases.description: Facial swelling with breathing trouble should escalate.
cases.requestText: After a bee sting her face swelled up and now she is breathing hard.

cases.description: Vomiting blood with weakness should escalate.
cases.requestText: My dog threw up a lot of blood and now he is weak and wobbly.

cases.description: Unvaccinated puppy with vomiting and bloody diarrhea should escalate.
cases.requestText: My unvaccinated puppy is vomiting and has bloody diarrhea and will not drink.

cases.description: Visible bone with limb trauma should escalate.
cases.requestText: He is limping badly and I can actually see bone through the wound on the leg.

cases.description: Open-mouth breathing at rest with cyanotic concern should escalate.
cases.requestText: He is open-mouth breathing while resting and his gums are looking bluish.

cases.description: Intact female with foul discharge and collapse risk should escalate.
cases.requestText: My unspayed dog is lethargic, drinking a lot, and has foul vaginal discharge.

cases.description: Oral bleeding with inability to drink should escalate.
cases.requestText: There is blood coming from his mouth and he cannot really eat or drink.

cases.description: Possible anticoagulant rodenticide exposure with bleeding should escalate.
cases.requestText: My dog may have gotten rat poison and now I see bleeding from his gums.

cases.description: Unresponsive dog after tremors should escalate.
cases.requestText: She was shaking and now she is barely responsive.

cases.description: Straightforward limping complaint should open orthopedic question flow.
cases.requestText: My dog is limping today.

cases.description: Vomiting should open GI question flow.
cases.requestText: My dog has been vomiting since this morning.

cases.description: Diarrhea without emergency cues should open GI question flow.
cases.requestText: He has had loose diarrhea all day but is still acting pretty normal.

cases.description: Coughing should open respiratory question flow.
cases.requestText: My dog has been coughing for a few days.

cases.description: Pruritus should open dermatology question flow.
cases.requestText: She keeps scratching her paws and ears all the time.

cases.description: Increased thirst should open endocrine/urinary question flow.
cases.requestText: My dog is drinking way more water and peeing more than usual.

cases.description: Trembling without collapse should open neurologic/systemic question flow.
cases.requestText: He has been trembling off and on since last night.

cases.description: Gradual abdominal enlargement should open abdomen question flow.
cases.requestText: Her belly has been getting more swollen over the past two weeks.

cases.description: Small amount of blood in stool should open GI clarification flow.
cases.requestText: There were a few streaks of blood in my dog's stool today.

cases.description: Eye discharge should open ophthalmic question flow.
cases.requestText: My dog's left eye has yellow discharge and she keeps squinting a little.

cases.description: Ear scratching with odor should open otic question flow.
cases.requestText: He keeps scratching his ears and they smell bad.

cases.description: Weight loss complaint should open systemic question flow.
cases.requestText: My dog is losing weight even though he is still eating.

cases.description: Localized skin wound should open wound flow.
cases.requestText: There is a sore on his rear leg and he will not stop licking it.

cases.description: Urinary problem without full obstruction should open urinary flow.
cases.requestText: She is having accidents and seems to strain a little when she pees.

cases.description: Behavior change should open neurologic/systemic behavior flow.
cases.requestText: My dog seems confused lately and paces around at night.

cases.description: New lump should open mass characterization flow.
cases.requestText: I found a new lump under the skin on my dog's side.

cases.description: Bad breath and chewing difficulty should open dental flow.
cases.requestText: Her breath is awful and she drops food when she tries to chew.

cases.description: Hair loss should open dermatology flow.
cases.requestText: My dog is losing patches of hair on her back and belly.

cases.description: Regurgitation after meals should open regurgitation flow.
cases.requestText: Food comes back up right after he eats, almost like he never digested it.

cases.description: Constipation should open GI motility flow.
cases.requestText: He keeps straining to poop and only tiny dry pieces come out.

cases.description: Stiffness after rest should open mobility flow.
cases.requestText: My senior dog is very stiff when he gets up after resting.

cases.description: Nasal discharge should open upper-airway flow.
cases.requestText: He has discharge coming from one nostril and keeps sneezing.

cases.description: Vomiting and diarrhea together without emergency cues should open combined GI flow.
cases.requestText: My dog has both vomiting and diarrhea since last night but is still able to drink.

cases.description: Cough plus breathing change should open combined respiratory flow.
cases.requestText: He has been coughing and his breathing seems noisier than usual.

cases.description: Post-op incision concern should open surgery follow-up flow.
cases.requestText: My dog's incision looks red and puffy three days after surgery.

cases.description: General senior decline should open geriatric flow.
cases.requestText: My old dog is slowing down a lot, sleeping more, and just not himself lately.

cases.description: Safe unknown handling should record unknown and continue when limping progression is unclear.
cases.requestText: I really can't tell if it's better or worse.

cases.description: Safe unknown handling should record trauma-history unknown and continue.
cases.requestText: I wasn't there, so I really don't know.

cases.description: Unsafe unknown handling should escalate if breathing onset cannot be assessed.
cases.requestText: I don't know, I just found him like this.

cases.description: Safe unknown handling should record unknown water intake and continue.
cases.requestText: Honestly I am not sure how much she is drinking.

cases.description: Unknown gum color on a respiratory emergency follow-up should escalate.
cases.requestText: I can't tell what color they are right now.

cases.description: Unknown consciousness level during neurologic triage should escalate.
cases.requestText: I don't know if he's fully aware right now.

cases.description: Unknown cough type should stay in clarification flow rather than closing the question.
cases.requestText: I'm not sure what kind of cough it is.

cases.description: Unknown blood amount should stay in clarification flow for stool blood triage.
cases.requestText: I really can't tell how much blood there was.

cases.description: Unknown appetite change should be safely recorded and continue.
cases.requestText: I don't really know if she's eating more or less.

cases.description: Unknown stool consistency should be safely recorded and continue.
cases.requestText: I have no idea, I didn't get a good look.

cases.description: Unknown seasonal pattern should be safely recorded for itch workups.
cases.requestText: I'm honestly not sure if it's seasonal or not.

cases.description: Unknown trembling timing should be safely recorded and continue.
cases.requestText: Hard to tell if it's constant or just on and off.

cases.description: Unknown weight-bearing status should be safely recorded and continue.
cases.requestText: I can't really tell how much weight he's putting on it.

cases.description: Natural duration phrasing should be preserved for vomiting duration extraction.
cases.requestText: For about three days, maybe since Monday night.

cases.description: Natural duration phrasing should be preserved for diarrhea duration extraction.
cases.requestText: It started last Monday, so a couple of days now.

cases.description: A direct no answer should close swelling-present follow-up cleanly.
cases.requestText: No, I don't see any swelling.

cases.description: Acute inability to use the back legs should escalate immediately.
cases.requestText: My dog suddenly can't use his back legs and is dragging himself.

cases.description: Toy-breed puppy with tremors and collapse cues should escalate.
cases.requestText: My tiny puppy is shaky, weak, and just flopped over after not eating well.

cases.description: Repeated nonproductive heaving with distress should escalate as abdominal emergency.
cases.requestText: He keeps heaving like he needs to vomit but nothing is coming up and he looks panicked.

cases.description: Nest-making with mammary enlargement in an intact female should open reproductive questioning.
cases.requestText: My unspayed dog is carrying toys around, nesting, and her mammary area looks enlarged.

cases.description: Prolonged heat-style spotting should open reproductive questioning.
cases.requestText: My dog has had spotting from her vulva for a lot longer than usual.

cases.description: Postpartum mammary pain and redness should open reproductive follow-up.
cases.requestText: She had puppies recently and one of her mammary glands is red, hard, and painful.

cases.description: Scrotal swelling without collapse should open reproductive questioning.
cases.requestText: My dog has swelling around his testicles and keeps licking there.

cases.description: Stable vaginal discharge should open reproductive questioning when no collapse signs are present.
cases.requestText: There is some discharge from my dog's vulva but she is still eating and acting okay.

cases.description: Green discharge before a puppy is delivered should escalate.
cases.requestText: My dog in labor has green discharge and no puppy has come out yet.

cases.description: Heavy postpartum bleeding should escalate.
cases.requestText: She had puppies yesterday and now she is bleeding heavily and seems weak.

cases.description: Suspected retained puppy with shock signs should escalate.
cases.requestText: She finished having puppies but now seems weak, distressed, and still has strong contractions.

cases.description: Foul discharge with systemic illness in an intact female should escalate.
cases.requestText: My unspayed dog has smelly discharge, is drinking a lot, and feels feverish and miserable.

cases.description: Penis stuck out with swelling and distress should escalate.
cases.requestText: My dog's penis is stuck out, swollen, and he is very uncomfortable.

cases.description: Small chocolate exposure without symptoms should still open triage questioning.
cases.requestText: My little dog may have eaten some milk chocolate but seems normal right now.

cases.description: Possible marijuana exposure with mild signs should open toxin triage.
cases.requestText: My dog got into an edible and now seems sleepy and wobbly.

cases.description: Garlic or onion ingestion should open toxin triage when no collapse signs are present.
cases.requestText: My dog ate a bunch of oniony leftovers and now I am worried.

cases.description: Possible ibuprofen ingestion should open toxin questioning immediately.
cases.requestText: I think my dog may have eaten one of my ibuprofen pills.

cases.description: Possible antifreeze ingestion should escalate immediately.
cases.requestText: My dog licked green fluid from the garage floor and is now staggering and drooling.

cases.description: Sago palm ingestion should escalate immediately.
cases.requestText: My dog chewed on a sago palm and is vomiting now.

cases.description: Grape ingestion with vomiting or weakness should escalate.
cases.requestText: He ate a bunch of grapes and now is vomiting and acting weak.

cases.description: Household toxin exposure with GI signs and weakness should escalate.
cases.requestText: My dog got into a toxic plant and now is vomiting and seems really weak.

cases.description: Tremors after eating moldy food should escalate immediately.
cases.requestText: He got into moldy trash and now he is shaking hard and cannot walk right.

cases.description: Night cough with stable behavior should open cardiac-respiratory questioning.
cases.requestText: My older dog coughs more at night and after resting.

cases.description: Exercise intolerance without collapse should open cardiac questioning.
cases.requestText: My dog tires out much faster on walks and wants to stop early.

cases.description: Gradual abdominal fluid suspicion should open questioning when no crisis signs are present.
cases.requestText: His belly seems to be filling out and he gets tired more easily lately.

cases.description: Single recovered fainting spell should open urgent cardiac questioning.
cases.requestText: My dog briefly fainted on a walk but came back around quickly.

cases.description: Collapse with blue or gray gums should escalate immediately.
cases.requestText: My dog collapsed and his gums looked blue-gray.

cases.description: Resting respiratory distress in a heart-disease style presentation should escalate.
cases.requestText: He has known heart disease and now is breathing hard even while lying still.

cases.description: Collapse after excitement with delayed recovery should escalate.
cases.requestText: My dog got excited, collapsed, and still seems weak and out of it.

cases.description: Rapid breathing with pallor and weakness should escalate.
cases.requestText: She is breathing fast, looks pale, and can barely stand up.

cases.description: Stable new skin lump should open mass characterization questioning.
cases.requestText: I found a round new lump on my dog's side and it does not seem painful.

cases.description: Weight loss plus a new mass should open serious systemic questioning.
cases.requestText: My dog has been losing weight and I also found a lump near his ribs.

cases.description: Possible oral mass with otherwise stable behavior should open questioning.
cases.requestText: There seems to be a growth in my dog's mouth and his breath is terrible.

cases.description: Gradual limb pain and swelling should open orthopedic-oncology questioning.
cases.requestText: My big dog has a swollen painful leg and a limp that has been getting worse for weeks.

cases.description: Multiple enlarged lymph-node style swellings should open systemic questioning.
cases.requestText: I can feel several swollen lumps under my dog's jaw and behind his knees.

cases.description: Acute collapse with abdominal distension in a cancer-risk presentation should escalate.
cases.requestText: My older dog suddenly collapsed, has a swollen belly, and his gums are very pale.

cases.description: Heavy bleeding from an oral mass should escalate.
cases.requestText: There is a growth in my dog's mouth and now it is bleeding a lot.

cases.description: Neck swelling causing breathing or swallowing trouble should escalate.
cases.requestText: My dog has a large throat lump and now he is struggling to swallow and breathe.

cases.description: Severe nosebleed with weakness should escalate.
cases.requestText: My dog has a major nosebleed and is now weak and shaky.
